content: {"analyses": [{"source_url": "https://arroma.uiowa.edu/docs/publication/paper_pdf/2025/xiao_et_al_2025.pdf", "source_title": "Implementation of Human-AI Interaction in Reinforcement Learning: Literature Review and Case Studies", "source_type": "web", "source_quality": 0.7, "overall_relevance": 0.65, "year": 0, "authors": [], "venue": "", "doi": "", "atomic_facts": [{"statement": "We have RLHF to thank for the fact that current LLMs produce helpful, humanlike text that largely avoids harmful or inappropriate responses.", "direct_quote": "We have RLHF to thank for the fact that current LLMs produce helpful, humanlike text that largely avoids harmful or inappropriate responses.", "fact_category": "statistic", "relevance_score": 0.65, "confidence": 0.88, "perspective": "Scientific", "entities": []}, {"statement": "First, the untuned or \u2018base\u2019 model is tested in a controlled environment.", "direct_quote": "First, the untuned or \u2018base\u2019 model is tested in a controlled environment.", "fact_category": "statistic", "relevance_score": 0.65, "confidence": 0.88, "perspective": "Scientific", "entities": []}, {"statement": "This data is presented to human annotators, who provide feedback, most commonly by ranking the model\u2019s outputs, labelling them as either \u2018go...", "direct_quote": "This data is presented to human annotators, who provide feedback, most commonly by ranking the model\u2019s outputs, labelling them as either \u2018good\u2019 or \u2018bad\u2019, or choosing between two answers to the same prompt.", "fact_category": "statistic", "relevance_score": 0.65, "confidence": 0.88, "perspective": "Scientific", "entities": []}], "evidence_summary": "Auto-extracted 3 facts."}]}
